question_text: Which of the following is a definition of energy conservation?

options.label: A
options.text: Energy can be created and destroyed during chemical reactions

options.label: B
options.text: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred or transformed

options.label: C
options.text: Energy always increases in a closed system

options.label: D
options.text: Energy is only found in living organisms

explanation: The law of conservation of energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, but can be transferred between objects or transformed from one form to another. This is a fundamental principle of physics.
